entries.title: About the Course
entries.content: Skills Focus: New skills introduced are simple perspective, landscape drawing, light source, shadows, tonal variations, and drawing board use. Charcoal is introduced as a new medium. Mathematical drawing is explored through tessellations. More complex color theory is explored with tertiary and complementary colors, an expanded color wheel, mixing browns, and value change in color. New watercolor skills: wash, graduated wash, and lifting. Continue strengthening skills with soft pastels. Brush drawing assignments span the year as opposed to one term.

entries.title: Placement & Combining Tips
entries.content:
If students are in grade 5 and below and have not had formal art lessons, they should begin with Level 1. However, those with prior art knowledge can take the Level 1-3 fast-track lessons and then continue with Level 4.

Prerequisite: Level 3 or appropriate skill proficiency.
